---
title: "A1_54007301"
author: "540007301"
date: "`r format(Sys.time(), '%d %B, %Y')`"
format:
  html:
    code-tools: true
    code-fold: true
    fig_caption: yes
    number_sections: yes
    embed-resources: true
    theme: flatly
    toc: true
    toc_depth: 4
    toc_float: true
execute:
  echo: true
  tidy: true
---
```{r loadpackages, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
library(Biobase)
library(limma)

theme_set(theme_bw())
x = readxl::read_excel(here("assignment", "DATA2x02_survey_2025_Responses.xlsx"))
knitr::write_bib(c(.packages(),
                   "knitr", "rmarkdown"), "report.bib")


```


## Introduction.

ATODE

# Part 1: Exploratory Analysis

This section illustrates the data pre-processing steps and lists the 20 most differentially expressed genes. 
Firstly, the data pre-processing consists of clarifying the nameing structure by 
- na/"" are treated as NA character in R
- "///" and "//" are treated as 


```{r}
load("GSE138043.RData")
gse138043 <- gse

load("GSE46474.RData")
gse46474 <- gse

extract_first_symbol <- function(x) {
  if (is.na(x) || x == "") return(NA_character_)
  parts <- unlist(strsplit(x, " /// "))
  first <- parts[1]
  fields <- unlist(strsplit(first, " // "))
  if (length(fields) >= 2) fields[2] else NA_character_
}

extract_first_symbol_gse46474 <- function(x) {
  if (is.na(x) || x == "") return(NA_character_)
  parts <- unlist(strsplit(x, " /// "))
  parts[1]
}
```


```{r}
expr1 <- exprs(gse138043)
pd1 <- pData(gse138043)
fd1 <- fData(gse138043)

expr2 <- exprs(gse46474)
pd2 <- pData(gse46474)
fd2 <- fData(gse46474)
```

```{r}
write.csv(expr1, "expr1.csv")
write.csv(pd1, "pd1.csv")
write.csv(fd1, "fd1.csv")
write.csv(expr2, "expr2.csv")
write.csv(pd2, "pd2.csv")
write.csv(fd2, "fd2.csv")
```


```{r}
fd2
```


```{r}
#gse138043

group1 <- factor(pd1[["rejection at 12months post transplant:ch1"]])
group1_clean <- factor(make.names(as.character(group1)))

design1 <- model.matrix(~ 0 + group1_clean)
colnames(design1) <- levels(group1_clean)

fit1 <- lmFit(expr1, design1)
cont1 <- makeContrasts(AR - non.AR, levels = design1)
#↑works as make.names changes the name to AR/non.AR
fit1 <- contrasts.fit(fit1, cont1)
fit1 <- eBayes(fit1)
res1 <- topTable(fit1, number = Inf, adjust.method = "BH")

res1$GeneSymbol <- vapply(fd1[rownames(res1), "gene_assignment"], extract_first_symbol, character(1))
res1 <- res1[!is.na(res1$GeneSymbol) & res1$GeneSymbol != "", ]
res1 <- res1[order(res1$adj.P.Val, -abs(res1$logFC)), ]
res1_unique <- res1[!duplicated(res1$GeneSymbol), ]

top20_table1 <- res1_unique[1:20, c("GeneSymbol", "logFC", "adj.P.Val", "P.Value", "t", "B")]
```



```{r}
#gse46474 analsys
group2 <- factor(pd2[["procedure status:ch1"]])
group2_clean <- factor(make.names(as.character(group2)))

design2 <- model.matrix(~ 0 + group2_clean)
colnames(design2) <- levels(group2_clean)

fit2 <- lmFit(expr2, design2)
cont2 <- makeContrasts(
  post.transplant.acute.rejection..AR. - post.transplant.non.rejection..NR.,
  levels = design2
)

fit2 <- contrasts.fit(fit2, cont2)
fit2 <- eBayes(fit2)
res2 <- topTable(fit2, number = Inf, adjust.method = "BH")

res2$GeneSymbol <- vapply(fd2[rownames(res2), "Gene Symbol"], extract_first_symbol_gse46474, character(1))
res2 <- res2[!is.na(res2$GeneSymbol) & res2$GeneSymbol != "", ]
res2 <- res2[order(res2$adj.P.Val, -abs(res2$logFC)), ]
res2_unique <- res2[!duplicated(res2$GeneSymbol), ]

top20_table2 <- res2_unique[1:20, c("GeneSymbol", "logFC", "adj.P.Val", "P.Value", "t", "B")]
```


```{r}
#the below shows that the data is continuouys and roughly log-scale. This means that the datasets are clean, and thus the use of limma is appropriate for differential expression method. However for gse138043, due to the imbalance in AR and non-AR the power of the analysis may be affected.

dim(expr1)
summary(as.vector(expr1))
table(group1_clean)

dim(expr2)
summary(as.vector(expr2))
table(group2_clean)
```

```{r}
boxplot(expr1, las = 2, main = "GSE138043")
boxplot(expr2, las = 2, main = "GSE46474")

#あとでqqplot足す
```

```{r}
top20_1 <- res1_unique$GeneSymbol[1:20]
top20_2 <- res2_unique$GeneSymbol[1:20]

intersect(top20_1, top20_2)
setdiff(top20_1, top20_2)
setdiff(top20_2, top20_1)
length(intersect(top20_1, top20_2))
```

```{r}
t.test(top20_1, top20_2)
```
